1.transcript:
Software Co / AI Money Discovery

0:11 - Team Member 1
Team Member 3, why does the otter keep on turning? Hello.

1:45 - Team Member 2
Hello. How are we? Hanging in there.

1:51 - Team Member 1
Uh-huh.

1:51 - Team Member 1
I can see. I can see. Yeah, I am okay. I am. I'm looking forward to this call.

2:04 - Team Member 2
Is Team Member 3 joining?

2:06 - Team Member 1
Yeah. Team Member 3 has just decided to make his presence felt.

2:11 - Team Member 3
I was doing very important password stuff.

2:15 - Unidentified Speaker
Right.

2:18 - Team Member 1
And what's the client's name?

2:20 - Team Member 1
Client. I did actually link his website, his blogs and his blogs as well. He did confirm that he was joining.

2:34 - Team Member 2
Is there a brief of the nature of the project?

2:40 - Team Member 1
I did a full discovery. The good thing about all of this is that Client is now here and he will take us through what his actual brief is. We've obviously met, but I've also got Team Member 2, who's our Head of Development, and George project manager lead on the call as well. So as discussed with you, the purpose of this call, it's basically a deeper dive discovery. The actual tech heads, nerds, I didn't say that, are also in the call now. They're basically here to take that more technical information to then be able to go away, workshop it and put together a more solid proposal, as I've discussed with you for the planning and prototyping, first of all, but then also with a broader estimate of what the development could also look like, You and I, we've had a bit of a chat in regards to what it is you're wanting to do, but I think it places a whole lot more value if you can give that quick 30-level as in you're traveling 30 levels, not just two, in regards to what your actual ideas are and yeah, take it from there.

4:22 - Client
So do you want me to get started now or? Yeah, yeah, go for it.

4:26 - Team Member 1
Unless you want to make yourself a coffee or something first, but you know.

4:29 - Client
No, no, no, all good. I'm good to go, I'm good to go. Look, basically, you know, I'm sure Team Member 1 would have probably, you know, give me a bit of a background, but obviously I've been in financial services for probably 20 years, in our own business AI money for about 10 now. What we do here is we do obviously wealth management in terms of financial planning, and we do home and investment loans as well. And we also help clients with property investment and buyers agency and whatnot. Obviously, there's a catchment of clients that we can help quite a lot. But what we also have noticed more and more is that, maybe you call it lower value or do it yourself type clients where what we're seeing from them is that, economically things are changing a lot for people in that it's more cashless, it's more digital. And the more and more people we meet with, they're less and less aware of where their stuff is at. So what obviously the idea was, I call it invisible money in that people have cashflow, they have super, they have different bank accounts and loans and stuff, but they don't actually know where it all is and they're not aware about it on a quick basis. They can't just come up with it like that. They have to go over here for their super, over there for the bank, over there for their loan, it's a bit of a mess, okay? So I started looking around, is there some sort of app that, you know, kind of can tie all this stuff together? And what I found mostly out there is more budgeting style apps, which just tracks cash flow, but doesn't really go much deeper than that. So basically, you know, what I thought about is, you know, can we create an app that kind of brings it all together? So it brings their property value in, brings their loan in, you know, brings their super in there, brings their cash flow in there, you can store dollars within that app as well. So just a complete wealth management, wealth creation app that you can obviously track everything in one place and obviously, you know, build on that too. So initially, you know, you can manage cashflow. You can see your asset values climbing based on integrations and APIs. You can see your home loan going down or any other loans that you have. From that data, we can potentially like to make suggestions to people in that, say for example, if the home value is worth a million and the mortgage is 500K, we can say you've got X amount of equity, you could do something like this with that equity if you wanted to, to help you build your wealth. If they had a bank account with say 50K in it and they've got a mortgage, it's not offset, it could be basically say to them, maybe a notification saying, you've got 50 grand in your bank account, it's not offsetting your loan. If you offset your home loan, you can save 15 years and 300 grand and interests, for example, stuff like that. So proactive little hints and tips along the way, but also cashflow tips. If they've got $2,000 surplus this month based on what's coming and what's gone out in cashflow, maybe the suggestion is if that money goes into this loan or this goal, this is how much quicker you're gonna pay it off or how much quicker you're gonna save towards that goal, for example, okay? Beyond that, I think you can obviously get a bit more complex in the background, but we may not lead with this part. There's also, you know, comparison options that we can add to it moving forward in the backend in that, you know, they can basically compare their home loans, their car insurance, just like compare the markets and find all the other stuff. But I just, I feel like with the, I guess, development of AI and technology these days, if they were to enter that information upfront, you know, why can't we basically prompt them before renewal and actually find those comparisons for them upfront and say, here are three more cost effective options for what you have. A home loan, we know the interest rate, cause it's obviously fed in via API. We know what other interest rates are for a loan to value ratio of something similar to what they have. Can we make those suggestions in there as well? So in a nutshell, that's kind of, I don't know, is that enough information? Do you need more than that? What are we trying to build?

8:34 - Team Member 2
No, it gives, it paints, yeah, it paints a decent sort of picture. And, you know, from, I mean, from what I understand, you are looking to become like a centralized financial intelligence systems, right? For the financial planning and wealth management and growth of the clients. Is this going to be only for AI money or are you looking productize it that anybody can sort of download and use?

9:03 - Client
We want to productize it. We want to be able to basically go beyond just our AI money clients. We want to basically have it as a general go-to-market sort of thing too.

9:14 - Team Member 2
Interesting, okay. So anybody can download the app, go through the onboarding process of profiling, creating the profile, adding sort of your assets like liabilities, et cetera. And the system will sort of then understand that user's complete financial position, and continue to understand that in real time. We could ask permissions to sort of stay connected with certain platforms, right? Also track and sort of optimize their asset liability and cashflow, I'm guessing. Manage property well. Analyze their current lending structures, if it's one or multiple. Make certain suggestions. Or on the app, if there is a financial planner attached to it, then there could be a financial planner's module to have some insights as to what they can guide or advise their clients on. I'm just trying to understand like how...

10:20 - Client
Potentially. So basically, overall, the way we would need to run it up front is more of a general advice basis. You obviously need a license for that, but it would be on a general advice basis. We can obviously provide an option within that if they want to take it and be more personalized and actually speak to planner, we can actually do that on the back end.

10:40 - Team Member 2
But on the upfront, overall, it would be an option that to connect with a planner or some packages or things that they can sort of subscribe and get get access to a planner and maybe a planning session or two planning sessions.

10:51 - Client
Exactly. So that that's definitely something we've always thought about. Initially, the idea was, you know, is, you know, this could be just for AI money clients as a lead generation aspect. But then we thought quite a lot broader than that. We think that the potential is probably greater. I mean, I mean, that's our opinion anyway, at the moment. Yeah, so basically, look, I've noticed, like I said to you, there's a lot of budgeting style apps, but nothing that kind of ties everything together, even to the point where they can, you know, save documents. There is one solution like this, but it is heavily marketed to planners only. It's called MyProsperity. That kind of has assets, liabilities, cashflow, all that in there. And there's even a document storage aspect with it, where you can obviously scan your documents, save them all in there, So when it comes to tax time, you've got access to all your documents in one place. But it's never, I mean, I haven't noticed it. I don't believe they go direct to market. They basically just go straight to financial planners or other brokers.

11:49 - Team Member 2
They're mainly for accountants and financial advisors.

11:53 - Client
They are. So they approach that sort of the professional aspect, if you like, And then they allow us to potentially on-sell it, but they charge us per user per month. To do that. And, you know, what they want to charge us doesn't really make sense. I don't know why they haven't gone to market. I mean, that's their strategy for whatever reason. I'm not sure, you know.

12:15 - Team Member 2
And they just position themselves more as a B2B product.

Well, I believe they've been actually taken over by NetWealth, which is an investment company that basically has super funds and investment. They have a platform. They've been taken over or bought out, if you if you like, and I'm assuming they're gonna tailor it to themselves. I think there was another money management app, which wasn't the same as what I'm suggesting called Money Brilliant, which Westpac bought out too. So they tend to buy them out and then they kind of dissolve and disappear. I think they take them into their own tech and basically just, I don't know if they rebranded them at Westpac or whatever they did. I'm not sure exactly what happened to it, but you basically, they bought them out and then they kind of just, that particular app ended in existence. And I believe they just have the Westpac app or whatever that is. All the banking apps, they just don't do what I'm suggesting. The banking apps obviously only track their stuff within the banking sort of environment that you have with them, but they don't integrate with others. And there's a few others as well that we can access as well. modules.name: Financial Dashboard
modules.description: A central command center providing a net worth overview, asset and liability breakdown, monthly cash flow summaries, debt summary, property portfolio overview, investment portfolio overview, and potentially a credit scoring system with notifications.

modules.name: Asset & Liability Management
modules.description: This module allows users to manage and track various assets like properties, investments (shares, ETFs, managed funds), superannuation, and other assets (e.g., cars, jewelry – with manual input). It also covers liability and debt management for home loans, investment loans, personal loans, credit cards, and Buy Now Pay Later accounts, including tracking interest, payments, and identifying refinancing opportunities.

modules.name: Cash Flow & Budget Intelligence
modules.description: A module for real-time tracking of income and expenses, integrating directly with user accounts and transaction feeds. It will automatically categorize spending, track income, enable budgeting, forecast expenses, manage subscriptions, and allow users to create and track savings goals.

modules.name: Product Comparison Marketplace
modules.description: This module will enable users to compare various financial products such as home loans, car insurance, other lending products, and general insurances. It aims to proactively prompt users before renewals and offer cost-effective options based on their financial data.

modules.name: AI Recommendation Engine
modules.description: An AI-native core component that provides smart, personalized, and self-learning financial recommendations for planning, budgeting, refinancing, debt consolidations, and savings optimization. It includes financial health scoring and scenario generation capabilities.

requirements.description: Provide a comprehensive, centralized view of all financial assets (property, super, bank accounts) and liabilities (loans) in one place.
Offer proactive, personalized suggestions and notifications for wealth building, such as equity utilization, offset account optimization, and surplus cash allocation.
Integrate with external financial data sources using APIs (e.g., property data, comparison tools) and data scraping (e.g., superannuation funds) to provide real-time information.
Include a feature for product comparison across various financial services (e.g., home loans, car insurance) and proactively suggest better options to users.
Allow users to store and organize financial documents within the app for easy access, especially during events like tax time.
The application must be designed to be productized for the general market, not just for AI Money's existing clients, with an intuitive onboarding process for new users.
Ensure the app presents complex financial information in an easy-to-understand and simplified manner for the end-user, avoiding overcomplication.

integrations.name: Domain / RP Data
integrations.description: To provide real-time property value data and insights for asset tracking.

integrations.name: Financial Institutions (Banks, Lenders)
integrations.description: For direct integration with bank accounts, home loans, investment loans, personal loans, and credit cards to track balances, transactions, and payments.

integrations.name: Superannuation Funds
integrations.description: To gather superannuation balances and potentially identify existing insurance policies, likely via data scraping due to current lack of open banking APIs.

integrations.name: Comparison Service APIs
integrations.description: To access and display comparison data for various financial products like home loans, car insurance, and other lending products.

constraints.description: The app must operate within general advice licensing regulations upfront, with an option to connect users to licensed financial planners for personalized advice.
Superannuation funds currently do not provide open banking APIs, necessitating data scraping for integration.
The solution must be presented in a simple and easy-to-understand way for the end-user, avoiding overcomplication.
The project will be executed in stages, starting with an MVP, and module selection for development will be based on budgetary requirements.

assumptions.description: The client is fully aware of the regulatory and compliance requirements for providing financial advice, even on a general basis, and will secure necessary licenses.
The 'planning and prototyping' phase will successfully define the detailed technical architecture, UI/UX, and a clear scope for an MVP, along with an accurate development estimate.
The client will provide necessary access, data, and feedback in a timely manner to facilitate the discovery, planning, and development phases.
The app will primarily target a broad general public audience, not exclusively high-net-worth individuals, requiring a user-friendly and accessible design.
The 'Software Co' possesses the necessary technical expertise in AI, financial technology, and mobile/web application development to build this complex platform.

unknowns.description: The specific launch timeline for the app, beyond 'as soon as we can' or 'ASAP'.
Detailed budgetary allocation for the full development phase of the project, as module selection will depend on it.
The exact strategy and resources for acquiring and onboarding users for the productized app once launched.
The full extent of compliance requirements (e.g., data privacy, financial regulations) that might make the planning phase longer or more complex.
The specific user interface and experience design for manually adding assets like cars or jewelry, where APIs are not available.
The official and final name of the app; 'My Money Mate' is a working title.
The specific dates and format for the upcoming proposal presentation.

questions.question: What is AI Money's target launch timeframe for the Minimum Viable Product (MVP), especially since module selection will be based on budgetary requirements?
questions.reason: The client stated they want to launch 'as soon as we can' / 'ASAP', but also listed 'the specific launch timeline for the app' and 'detailed budgetary allocation for the full development phase' as unknowns, with 'module selection for development will be based on budgetary requirements' being a constraint.

questions.question: Can AI Money provide an estimated budget range or preferred investment level for the initial MVP development phase to help guide module prioritization?
questions.reason: The client noted that 'module selection for development will be based on budgetary requirements,' and 'detailed budgetary allocation for the full development phase of the project' is an unknown.

questions.question: The requirements list 'Document Storage' as a functional requirement, but it's not explicitly defined as a separate module. Should this be a distinct module, or a feature integrated into other modules?
questions.reason: A requirement states 'Allow users to store and organize financial documents within the app,' but 'Document Storage' is not present in the 'modules' list.

questions.question: Beyond general advice licensing, what specific financial regulatory compliance (e.g., data privacy, consumer protection) does AI Money anticipate for a productized app targeting the general market?
questions.reason: The client mentioned operating 'within general advice licensing regulations upfront' as a constraint, and 'the full extent of compliance requirements' is listed as an unknown.

questions.question: For the 'Product Comparison Marketplace' and 'AI Recommendation Engine', does AI Money have preferred comparison service APIs or data providers they intend to integrate with, or specific criteria for selecting them?
questions.reason: The client mentioned 'comparison options' and the availability of 'APIs that we can obviously take advantage of for that sort of comparison aspect,' and Team Member 2 suggested 'AI recommendations based on how you're going this month' related to the marketplace.

questions.question: Can AI Money provide any existing preferences or examples for the user interface and experience design regarding the manual input of assets like cars, jewelry, or other items where direct API integration isn't possible?
questions.reason: The client noted 'There'll be some manual add-ins they need to do, like your, you know, your cars and stuff' and 'The specific user interface and experience design for manually adding assets' is listed as an unknown.

questions.question: In the 'AI Recommendation Engine', are there specific initial types of recommendations (e.g., debt reduction, savings optimization, refinancing) that are considered higher priority for the MVP?
questions.reason: Team Member 2 broadly mentioned 'recommendations of planning, budgeting, refinancing, etc. Consolidations, savings optimization,' and the project will be 'executed in stages, starting with an MVP,' requiring prioritization.

questions.question: The 'Financial Dashboard' description includes 'potentially a credit scoring system with notifications.' Is this a definite requirement for the MVP, or a planned feature for a later stage?
questions.reason: Team Member 2 mentioned 'maybe some credit scoring system on maybe some notifications' during the brainstorming of the Financial Dashboard, implying a level of uncertainty or prioritization needed.

questions.question: Since the app will be productized for the general market, what initial user acquisition or onboarding strategy does AI Money envision to inform the design and technical infrastructure?
questions.reason: The client confirmed wanting to 'productize it' and have it as a 'general go-to-market sort of thing,' but 'the exact strategy and resources for acquiring and onboarding users' is listed as an unknown.

questions.question: While 'My Money Mate' is currently a working title, when does AI Money anticipate confirming the official and final name of the app to proceed with branding and legal checks?
questions.reason: The client stated, 'I'm thinking is my money mate,' and 'The official and final name of the app; 'My Money Mate' is a working title' is listed as an unknown.

3.sow:
# Scope of Work — My Money Mate

## 1. Executive Summary
Software Co has been engaged by AI Money to develop "My Money Mate," a comprehensive, AI-native financial management platform. The purpose of this project is to provide users with a centralized command center to track assets, liabilities, and cash flow while utilizing an AI recommendation engine to optimize wealth building. The value proposition lies in its ability to productize complex financial data into a simplified, user-friendly interface that offers proactive suggestions for refinancing, debt consolidation, and savings, ultimately democratizing sophisticated financial planning for the general market.

## 2. In-Scope Items
- **Financial Dashboard**: Centralized view of net worth, cash flow, and portfolio summaries.
- **Asset & Liability Management**: Tracking for properties, shares, superannuation, and various loan types.
- **Cash Flow & Budget Intelligence**: Automated expense categorization, income tracking, and goal setting.
- **Product Comparison Marketplace**: Tools to compare home loans, insurance, and lending products.
- **AI Recommendation Engine**: Personalized financial health scoring and scenario generation.
- **Document Storage**: A secure repository for financial documents and tax preparation.
- **User Onboarding**: Intuitive flow for new user registration and initial data integration.

## 3. Out-of-Scope Items
- **Direct Financial Advice**: The app provides general advice only; personalized licensed financial planning is excluded from the software's automated functions.
- **Manual Marketing/User Acquisition**: While the infrastructure supports it, the execution of marketing campaigns is out of scope.
- **Legal/Regulatory Licensing**: Securing AFSL or other financial licenses is the responsibility of AI Money.
- **Direct API Development for Superannuation**: Due to industry limitations, only data scraping is in scope for superannuation funds.

## 4. Modules and Deliverables

### Module: Financial Dashboard
- **Priority**: High
- **Features**: Net worth overview, monthly cash flow summary, debt summary, credit scoring notifications.
- **Acceptance Criteria**: Dashboard must display aggregated data from all linked accounts within 5 seconds of login; visuals must clearly distinguish between assets and liabilities.

### Module: Asset & Liability Management
- **Priority**: High
- **Features**: Property portfolio tracking, investment (shares/ETFs) tracking, manual asset entry (jewelry/cars), loan tracking (Home, BNPL, Credit Cards).
- **Acceptance Criteria**: System must support manual input for non-API assets and successfully pull real-time property valuations via RP Data/Domain APIs.

### Module: Cash Flow & Budget Intelligence
- **Priority**: High
- **Features**: Real-time transaction categorization, subscription management, savings goal tracking, expense forecasting.
- **Acceptance Criteria**: Transactions must be automatically categorized with at least 85% accuracy before user correction; users can set and track at least 3 concurrent savings goals.

### Module: AI Recommendation Engine
- **Priority**: High
- **Features**: Financial health scoring, equity utilization alerts, refinancing prompts, scenario generation.
- **Acceptance Criteria**: AI must generate at least one proactive recommendation monthly based on surplus cash or high-interest debt detection.

### Module: Product Comparison Marketplace
- **Priority**: Medium
- **Features**: Home loan and insurance comparison tool, renewal alerts.
- **Acceptance Criteria**: Comparison results must refresh via API and display a minimum of three competitive options for any queried product type.

### Module: Document Storage
- **Priority**: Medium (Requirement-based)
- **Features**: Document categorization, secure upload, tax-time folder organization.
- **Acceptance Criteria**: Users can successfully upload, preview, and categorize PDFs/images; documents must be encrypted at rest.

## 5. Integrations

- **System**: Domain / RP Data
  - **Purpose**: Real-time property valuations.
  - **Type**: REST API
  - **Data Flow**: Inbound (Property values and market trends to My Money Mate).

- **System**: Financial Institutions (Banks/Lenders)
  - **Purpose**: Transactional and balance tracking.
  - **Type**: Open Banking API / Aggregator
  - **Data Flow**: Inbound (Balances and transaction history to My Money Mate).

- **System**: Superannuation Funds
  - **Purpose**: Balance and insurance policy tracking.
  - **Type**: Data Scraping
  - **Data Flow**: Inbound (Fund data to My Money Mate).

- **System**: Comparison Service APIs
  - **Purpose**: Access to live financial product rates.
  - **Type**: REST API
  - **Data Flow**: Inbound (Rates and product details to Marketplace module).

## 6. Constraints and Assumptions

### Constraints
- **Compliance**: Must strictly adhere to general advice licensing to avoid legal repercussions for providing unlicensed personal advice.
- **Technical**: Superannuation data is limited to scraping due to a lack of Open Banking APIs in that sector.
- **Design**: The UI must be simplified for a general audience, preventing data-heavy "over-complication."
- **Budgetary**: Development is phased; modules will be built based on specific budget allocations per stage.

### Assumptions
- **Licensing**: AI Money will secure all necessary financial and data privacy licenses.
- **Data Access**: Client will provide necessary sandbox environments or credentials for third-party integrations.
- **Expertise**: Software Co is assumed to have full capacity for AI and Fintech-specific development.
- **Discovery Phase**: It is assumed the planning phase will result in a definitive MVP roadmap.

## 7. Open Items
- **Timeline**: Confirmed; Phase 2 (MVP) duration is 12 weeks. Final launch set for Week 18.
- **Budget**: Detailed budgetary allocation per module is required to finalize the development sequence.
- **Module Definition**: Confirm if 'Document Storage' remains a standalone module or is integrated into 'Asset Management.'
- **Credit Scoring**: Determine if credit scoring is a Day-1 MVP requirement or a Phase 2 feature.
- **Asset UX**: Define the specific UI/UX flow for manual entry of high-value personal assets (e.g., jewelry).

## 8. Timeline Overview
- **Phase 1: Planning & Prototyping (Weeks 1-2)**: Technical architecture, UI/UX design, and MVP scope finalization.
- **Phase 2: MVP Development (Weeks 3-14)**: 12-week development cycle for High-Priority modules (Dashboard, Cash Flow, Assets).
- **Phase 3: AI & Marketplace Integration (Weeks 15-16)**: Recommendation Engine and Comparison Marketplace integration.
- **Phase 4: UAT & Launch (Weeks 17-18)**: User testing, compliance audit, and public release.
changelog: Extended the MVP Development phase (Phase 2) to a 12-week duration as per user feedback (previously an 8-week allocation).
Condensed the total project timeline from 26 weeks to 18 weeks by streamlining non-development phases.
Adjusted Phase 1 (Planning) from 4 weeks to 2 weeks to align with the revised delivery schedule.
Shortened Phase 3 and Phase 4 durations to 2 weeks each to maintain project momentum post-MVP development.
Updated Section 7 (Open Items) to reflect that the timeline has been firm and is no longer 'ASAP'.

tasks.title: Cloud Infrastructure and Scalable Database Setup
tasks.description: Establish the foundational cloud environment including AWS/Azure services, relational databases for user data, and high-performance caching for financial feeds. This ensures the system can handle concurrent API requests from multiple aggregators.
acceptance_criteria: Infrastructure as Code (IaC) templates are successfully deployed.
Database is accessible with encryption at rest enabled.

tasks.title: User Authentication and Multi-Step Onboarding Flow
tasks.description: Develop a secure login system with MFA and an intuitive onboarding wizard that captures basic user profile information and financial goals. This is the entry point for all new users to the platform.
acceptance_criteria: User can register and verify account via email/MFA.
Onboarding flow persists data across sessions until completion.

tasks.title: Financial Data Aggregator Integration (Open Banking)
tasks.description: Implement the integration layer with providers like Basiq or Yodlee to fetch bank account balances and transaction histories. This serves as the primary data source for the platform.
acceptance_criteria: System successfully authenticates and pulls data from a sandbox bank account.
Token refresh logic is implemented to maintain long-term data access.

tasks.title: Core Dashboard UI and Layout Implementation
tasks.description: Build the main interface shell to host the financial widgets, including navigation and the Net Worth summary header. The UI must be responsive and follow the simplified design philosophy.
acceptance_criteria: Dashboard skeleton loads in under 2 seconds.
Navigation menu works correctly on mobile and desktop.

tasks.title: Property Valuation API Integration (RP Data/Domain)
tasks.description: Develop the connection to real-time property valuation services to provide users with automated equity estimates. Users should be able to search for addresses and link them to their portfolio.
acceptance_criteria: Property valuations are returned for valid Australian addresses.
Market trend data is stored and updateable.

tasks.title: Transaction Categorization Engine
tasks.description: Build a categorization logic that uses ML or rule-based processing to group transactions into categories like Groceries, Transport, and Subscriptions. This must meet the 85% accuracy requirement.
acceptance_criteria: 85% of sample transaction data is correctly categorized automatically.
Users can manually override categories.

tasks.title: Manual Asset and Liability Entry System
tasks.description: Create forms for users to manually input assets like jewelry, vehicles, and BNPL debts that cannot be tracked via API. This ensures a complete net worth calculation.
acceptance_criteria: Users can add, edit, and delete manual asset entries.
Manual entries are correctly factored into total net worth.

tasks.title: Superannuation Data Scraping Service
tasks.description: Develop a specialized service to scrape balance and insurance data from major superannuation providers where Open Banking is unavailable. This is a critical component for long-term wealth tracking.
acceptance_criteria: Service successfully extracts balance and policy details from at least two test funds.
Error handling handles login failures or captcha prompts gracefully.

tasks.title: Cash Flow Forecasting and Goal Tracking
tasks.description: Implement logic to predict future cash flow based on historical spending and allow users to set specific savings targets. The system must track progress against these goals in real-time.
acceptance_criteria: User can set at least 3 concurrent savings goals.
Forecast visuals correctly project 6-month spending trends.

tasks.title: Financial Health Scoring Model
tasks.description: Develop the AI model that calculates a proprietary health score based on debt-to-income ratios, savings rates, and asset growth. This score serves as the baseline for recommendations.
acceptance_criteria: Health score updates automatically when new financial data is synced.
The model correctly flags high-interest debt.

tasks.title: Scenario Generation and Refinancing Prompts
tasks.description: Build an AI feature that generates 'what if' scenarios (e.g., impact of an extra $500 mortgage repayment). Proactively prompt users when equity utilization or refinancing opportunities are detected.
acceptance_criteria: User receives an alert when their equity allows for a lower interest rate product.
Scenario UI displays comparison of current vs. proposed financial paths.

tasks.title: Marketplace API Integration and Comparison Tool
tasks.description: Integrate third-party comparison APIs to pull live rates for home loans and insurance. Build the UI for users to compare their current rates against market alternatives.
acceptance_criteria: Display a minimum of three competitive options for home loans.
Renewal alerts trigger 30 days before insurance expiration.

tasks.title: Secure Document Storage and Encryption
tasks.description: Implement a document repository for PDFs and images (e.g., tax receipts, loan contracts). Files must be encrypted at rest and organized by category.
acceptance_criteria: Users can upload and preview PDF files within the application.
Documents are inaccessible without a valid user session and decrypted only on demand.

sprints.name: Sprint 1 — Technical Foundation & Onboarding
sprints.goal: Establish the cloud environment, database, and user authentication flow to begin secure data handling.

sprints.name: Sprint 2 — Core Data Aggregation
sprints.goal: Integrate primary banking and property APIs to start populating the application with real financial data.

sprints.name: Sprint 3 — Cash Flow & Asset Tracking
sprints.goal: Launch the categorization engine and manual entry features to provide a complete view of assets and spending.

sprints.name: Sprint 4 — Complex Assets & Forecasting
sprints.goal: Implement superannuation scraping and advanced budget forecasting tools to finalize the MVP data layer.

sprints.name: Sprint 5 — AI Engine & Health Scoring
sprints.goal: Build the intelligent layer that provides health scores and automated financial recommendations.

sprints.name: Sprint 6 — Marketplace & Document Management
sprints.goal: Complete the secondary modules including product comparisons and secure document storage for tax preparation.

issues.summary: Cloud Infrastructure and Scalable Database Setup
User Authentication and Multi-Step Onboarding Flow
Core Dashboard UI and Layout Implementation
Manual Asset and Liability Entry System
Cash Flow Forecasting and Goal Tracking
Marketplace API Integration and Comparison Tool
